sutta: the exalted 1 addressed the venerable udayan saying how many states are there who dying of ever minding now when he had spoken thus the vulnerable udain was silent and a second and a third time the exalted 1 spoke in light manner but the venerable udayan remained silent then said the venable ananda to the venerable udain udain renable sir the master addresses you and udain said i am listening to the exalted 1 ananda renable sir lord among remembers many previous existences that is to say 1 birth 2 birds and so on he remembers many previous as existences in all the modes in all the detail this lawn is a state of ever minding then said the exalted 1 i knew ananda this foolish fellow odyn did not live intent on higher thought how many states of ever minding are there ananda lord there are 5 states what 5 hearing lord among allu from sensual desires enters and abides in the first jhana the second jhana in the third jannah this state of ever minding lord thus may become thus made to increase leads to dwelling at ease here and now again lord he concentrates on the thought of light fixes his mind on the thought of day as by day so by night as by night so by day thus with mind neither hampered nor hindered he makes his mind become radiant this state of ever minding lord thus made become thus made to increase leads to the gain of inside knowledge niyana dasana again lord he considers this body upwards from the soles of his feet downwards from the hair of his head as skin bound as full of diverse impurities there are in this body the hair of the head the hair of the body nails teeth skin flesh sinews bones marrow kidney heart liver pleura spleen lungs intestines misentry belly customer bile phlegm pus blood sweat fat tears grease [Music] or limp spittles not synovia or oil of the joint urine this state of ever minding lord thus may become thus made to increase leads to the riddance of passionate lust again lord suppose he see a body cast away in a cemetery 1 day dead 2 days dead or 3 days dead bloated blue black a mass of past he com he compares his own body thus this body too is subject thus thus it will come to be this is not past or suppose he sees such a body being eaten by crows ravens vultures dogs jackals vermin he compares his own body in light manner so too a body that is a chain of bones with flesh and blood is a newbound or fleshless but blood dispatched sinew bound or without flesh or blood she knew bound all but bones scattered here and there here a hand bone there a foot bone there a leg bone here a thigh bone here a hip bone there a back here a skull should he see a body cast away in a cemetery the bones of which are white as a sea shell a heap of bones a rotting powdering mess years old he compares his own body thus this body too is subject thus thus it will come to be this is not past the state of ever minding lord thus made become thus made to increase leads to the rooting out of the conceit i am then lord putting by pleasure among enters and abides in the fourth jannah this state of ever-minding lord thus made become thus made to increase leads to the complete penetration of the countless elements lord there are these 5 states of ever minding sadhu sadhu ananda well done well done ananda and hold to this sixth to be a state of ever minding here in ananda among goes out mindful comes in mindful stands mindful sits mindful lies down mindful and is mindful in performing action this state of ever minding ananda thus may become thus made to increase leads to mindfulness and self-possession that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this is a very good suta here there are 5 states of ever minding or mindfulness that uh remember ananda said the first 1 is the 3 jhanas which leads to a dwelling at ease here and now [Music] and the second 1 is he concentrates on the thought of light fixes his mind on the thought of day as by day so by night as by night so by day thus with mind neither hampered nor hindered it makes his mind become radiant and this leads to inside knowledge so you see this is the best condition for a person to obtain nyana dasana seeing sometimes is translated as seeing and knowing sometimes it's translated as inside knowledge so from here you can understand the purpose of samadhi the purpose of jhana why jana is very often stressed in the buddha's discourses and the reason is jhana literally is a state of mental incandescence when the mind becomes very bright and here we know why the buddha wants us to cultivate the mind very bright because it leads to inside knowledge if the mind is dull it's not bright now it does not lead to uh inside knowledge then the third thing that mentioned is the meditation on the 32 parts of the body and here it is said that the meditation on the 32 parts of the body leads to the riddance of passionate lust you loss is abandoned this way this meditation on the 32 parts of the body is both a samatha and a vipassana practice a tranquilization practice as well as a contemplation practice in the earlier utah we read angutta nikaya 2.3.10 it is said that samatha leads to the riddance of passionate last samatha just plain samadhi itself when the mind becomes very strong very strongly concentrated now the hindrances are abandoned and passionate lust is 1 of the hindrances so this is another way to get rid of passionate lust by contemplation on the 32 parts of the body then the fourth 1 is meditation on the cops the different types of corpse a corpse that is 3 days old bloated blue black a mess of past etc then when we think of this type of corpse and then we think of our own body 1 day our body will also be like that foul smelling rotting or a few days later it is broken up eaten up by crows vultures dogs worms etc so in the same way we also think that 1 day our body will be like that or after some time when the most of the flesh is eaten up then it is a chain of bones with some flesh left some blood left and the sinews are still there and binding all the bones together oh it is fleshless no more flesh but still some blood on it and the sinews are still there binding holding all the bones together or later without flesh or blood and then later when the sinews are also gone and then the bones are scattered here and there here a hand bone there a foot bone tear a leg bone etc then later the bones become white as a sea shell and and later even turns to powder yeah so when we think about how this body is going to end up that way it helps us to get rid of the conceit i am then the fifth 1 is the fourth jhana and is stated here the fourth jana leads to the complete penetration of the countless elements the fourth jana is superior to the earlier 3 janus and because it is such a tranquil state that deep inside can be obtained from the fourth ghana and then the sixth 1 that the buddha mentioned is mindfulness of our body in the sense of that among goes up mindful comes in mindful stance mindful sits mindful lies down mindful and is mindful in performing action this leads to mindfulness and self-possession by itself it doesn't give you insight when you are mindful of your bodily actions and by itself it does not lead us to much wisdom but it maintains our mindfulness and self-possession sati samba janiya and if we can maintain our mindfulness without it being broken then it leads to concentration to a low stage of concentration and it helps us to develop samadhina this uh in the satipatana there are various contemplations in the body and the earlier ones are more useful like the 32 parts of the body the cops and all that because it um especially the corpse 1 it gives us some urgency to practice let's stop here for a while and the next suta is
